name: sqlitestudio
title: sqlitestudio
summary: free, open source, multi-platform SQLite database manager
description:
free, open source, multi-platform SQLite database manager
SQLiteStudio is desktop application for browsing and editing SQLite database
files. It is aimed for people, who know what SQLite is, or what relational
databases are in general.

commands.name: sqlitestudio
commands.description: GUI interface to SQLiteStudio, a SQLite manager

arguments.description: Enables debug messages in console (accessible with F12).

arguments.description: Enables debug messages in console (accessible with F12).
arguments.raw: --debug  Enables debug messages in console (accessible with F12).

arguments.description: Enables Lemon parser debug messages for SQL code assistant.
arguments.raw: --debug-lemon  Enables Lemon parser debug messages for SQL code assistant.

arguments.description: Enables debugging of SQLiteStudio's query executor.
arguments.raw: --debug-query-executor  Enables debugging of SQLiteStudio's query executor.

arguments.description: Enables debugging of every single SQL query being sent to any database.
arguments.raw: --debug-sql  Enables debugging of every single SQL query being sent to any database.

arguments.description: Redirects debug messages into standard output (forces debug mode).
arguments.raw: --debug-stdout  Redirects debug messages into standard output (forces debug mode).

arguments.description: Displays help on commandline options.
Displays help on commandline options.

arguments.raw: --help-all  Displays help including Qt specific options.

arguments.description: Lists plugins installed in the SQLiteStudio and quits.
arguments.raw: --list-plugins  Lists plugins installed in the SQLiteStudio and quits.

arguments.description: Displays version information.

commands.name: sqlitestudiocli
commands.description: Command line interface to SQLiteStudio, a SQLite manager

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sqlitestudio because free, open source, multi-platform SQLite database manager.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sqlitestudio as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqlitestudio [options] file` as documented upstream for sqlitestudio.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sqlitestudio with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sqlitestudio --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
